title: Easy Chocolate Granola
excerptHtml: Easy Chocolate Granola Nutty, chocolaty, and wholesome, this granola is a delicious snack for kids and adults alike. Healthy, filling,...
contentHtml:
Easy Chocolate Granola

Nutty, chocolaty, and wholesome, this granola is a delicious snack for kids and adults alike. Healthy, filling, and easy enough for little hands to help make!

About the Recipe

This crunchy chocolate granola is the perfect mix of indulgence and nutrition — packed with nuts, seeds, and oats for energy, with a hint of cocoa to make it irresistible. Enjoy it as a quick breakfast, a topping for yogurt, or just as a snack straight from the jar!

Ingredients

Oats – 1 cup (90 grams)
Chopped nuts* – 3/4 cup (100 grams) (almonds/walnuts/pistachios)
Mixed seeds – 2 tbsp (flaxseeds, chia, pumpkin)
Desiccated coconut – 2 tbsp
Cocoa powder – 1 tbsp
Cinnamon powder – 1/2 tsp
Honey – 1 tbsp
Melted ghee/coconut oil – 1.5 tbsp
Mixed dried fruits – 3 tbsp (cranberries/figs/raisins)
Chocolate chips – 2 tbsp

Instructions

  1. In a pan, add the measured oats and dry roast for 5–7 minutes. This step is optional but helps to make the granola crunchier.
  2. In a large bowl, mix together the roasted oats, chopped nuts, seeds, coconut, cocoa powder, cinnamon, honey, and ghee.
  3. Stir well and transfer to a baking tray lined with parchment paper.
  4. Bake in a preheated oven at 160°C for 15 minutes, then remove the tray, stir the granola, and continue to bake for another 15 minutes.
  5. Once slightly cool, add the dried fruits and mix well.

Notes

Serving suggestion – Serve with milk or yogurt for breakfast, or enjoy plain as a crunchy snack.
Storage – Store in an airtight jar at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.
Tip – Add chocolate chips only after the granola has cooled slightly to prevent melting.

title: Date Cookies
excerptHtml: Date Cookies Soft, naturally sweet, and nutty, these date cookies are a wholesome treat perfect for any time of day....
contentHtml:
Date Cookies

Soft, naturally sweet, and nutty, these date cookies are a wholesome treat perfect for any time of day. Bursting with flavor, they’re both healthy and indulgent!

About the Recipe

These cookies strike the perfect balance between health and indulgence — chewy from the dates, crunchy from the walnuts, and naturally sweet without being heavy. They’re the kind of bake that pairs beautifully with your evening tea or makes a nourishing snack on the go.

Ingredients

Butter – 28 grams
Brown sugar – 40 grams
Dates pulp – 1/4 cup
Flour – 140 grams
Milk powder – 1 tbsp
Baking powder – 1 tsp
Baking soda – 1/4 tsp
Milk – quantity sufficient
Walnuts – 15 grams

For Dates Pulp
Seedless dates – 20 grams
Warm water – 180–200 grams

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, add butter and brown sugar and whisk together.
  2. Add dates pulp and mix well.
  3. Add flour, milk powder, baking powder, and baking soda.
  4. Then add walnuts.
  5. Adjust milk quantity accordingly to form a soft dough.
  6. Bake at 160°C for 10 minutes.
  7. For dates pulp:
  8. Soak 20 grams of seedless dates in 180–200 grams of warm water for half an hour.
  9. Blend the softened dates to form a smooth paste.

Notes

Serving suggestion – Pair with a cup of masala chai or coffee for a cozy, naturally sweet snack.
Storage – Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.
Tip – Toast the walnuts lightly before adding for an extra nutty crunch.

title: Coffee Cinnamon Pudding
excerptHtml: Coffee Cinnamon Pudding Celebrate the festive season with this silky, eggless coffee cinnamon pudding — creamy, comforting, and full of...
contentHtml:
Coffee Cinnamon Pudding

Celebrate the festive season with this silky, eggless coffee cinnamon pudding — creamy, comforting, and full of holiday vibes!

About the Recipe

This pudding brings together the warmth of cinnamon and the bold flavor of coffee in a smooth, luscious dessert. It’s the perfect balance of cozy and elegant — easy enough for a weeknight treat yet impressive enough for festive gatherings.

Ingredients

Milk – 1.5 cups (360 ml)
Sugar – 1/4 cup (50 g)
Cinnamon powder – 1 tsp
Cocoa powder – 2 tsp
Cornflour – 3 tbsp
Brewed coffee/espresso – 100 ml
Walnuts or almonds, roasted & chopped – 2 tbsp

Instructions

  1. Brew the espresso using your favourite coffee capsule.
  2. In a saucepan, combine all the ingredients and whisk well to ensure no lumps form.
  3. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring in intervals.
  4. Add the chopped walnuts or almonds and stir.
  5. Switch off the flame and divide the pudding into serving bowls.
  6. Refrigerate for 45 minutes. Once set, garnish with whipped cream, berries, chocolate chips, and cocoa powder. Enjoy!

Notes

Serving suggestion – Top with whipped cream, cocoa dust, or chocolate curls for a café-style finish.
Storage – Refrigerate in covered bowls for up to 2 days.
Tip – Use freshly brewed espresso for the best flavor and aroma.

title: Coconut Macaroon Cookies
excerptHtml: Sweet, chewy, and packed with coconut goodness, these macaroons are a tropical delight in every bite. Easy to make and...
contentHtml:
Sweet, chewy, and packed with coconut goodness, these macaroons are a tropical delight in every bite. Easy to make and perfect for snacking or gifting!

About the Recipe

Crisp on the outside, soft and chewy inside — these coconut macaroons are little bites of sunshine. With just a few pantry ingredients, you can whip up these golden, melt-in-your-mouth cookies that make any tea-time special or gifting occasion extra sweet.

Ingredients

Dessicated coconut – 90 grams
Condensed milk – 100 grams
Flour – 35 grams
Baking powder – 1/4 tsp
Milk – 90–100 ml

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, mix condensed milk and dessicated coconut.
  2. Add flour and baking powder.
  3. Add milk as needed to form a soft dough.
  4. Pipe the cookies on a silicon mat.
  5. Bake at 170°C for 8–10 minutes.
  6. Store in an airtight container for up to 1 week.

Notes

Serving suggestion – Enjoy these macaroons with a hot cup of coffee or drizzle them with melted chocolate for an indulgent touch.
Storage – Store in an airtight container for up to 1 week at room temperature.
Tip – For extra flavor, add a splash of vanilla extract or a sprinkle of toasted coconut on top before baking.

title: Candied Oranges
excerptHtml: Candied Oranges Sweet, tangy, and perfectly glossy — these homemade candied oranges make a delightful snack or a beautiful...
contentHtml:
Candied Oranges

Sweet, tangy, and perfectly glossy — these homemade candied oranges make a delightful snack or a beautiful garnish for desserts.

About the Recipe

These glistening slices of orange sunshine are a treat on their own or as a stunning topping for cakes, tarts, and puddings. With their chewy texture and citrusy sweetness, they’re as pretty as they are delicious — and surprisingly easy to make at home!

Ingredients

Oranges – 2
Water – approx 4 cups
Caster sugar – 2 cups

Instructions

  1. Slice the oranges to approx 1/8 inch thick. Don’t make them too thin or too thick.
  2. In a saucepan, add 2 cups of water and the orange slices and bring to a boil. Simmer for 2–4 minutes, then remove the slices and put them in ice-cold water.
  3. In the same or another saucepan, add 2 cups of water and 2 cups of caster sugar. Let it come to a boil, then transfer all the orange slices from the cold water to the saucepan.
  4. On a low flame, let the oranges simmer in the sugar water for approx 1 hour. After every 15 minutes, use a spoon to swirl the slices and pour the sugar water over them to ensure an even coating.
  5. Once the oranges look slightly translucent, remove them from the water and place them on a wire rack or a parchment-lined tray.
  6. Let them dry overnight, then bake in an oven at 100°C for 20 minutes on each side.
  7. Dust with caster sugar, then transfer to an airtight container.

Notes

Serving suggestion – Use as a garnish for cakes, cupcakes, and desserts, or enjoy as a sweet citrus snack.
Storage – Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.
Tip – For extra shine and flavor, brush the candied slices lightly with the leftover syrup before serving.

title: Blueberry Mug Cake
excerptHtml: A quick, soft, and fruity treat — this blueberry mug cake is perfect for satisfying your dessert cravings in just...
contentHtml:
A quick, soft, and fruity treat — this blueberry mug cake is perfect for satisfying your dessert cravings in just minutes!

About the Recipe

This single-serve dessert is everything you need when the sweet tooth strikes — fluffy, buttery, and bursting with juicy blueberries. Ready in under two minutes, it’s comfort in a mug without the wait or the mess!

Ingredients

Flour – 1/4 cup
Caster sugar – 3 tbsp
Baking powder – 1/2 tsp
Butter – 2 tbsp
Milk – 2 tbsp
Blueberries – 1 tbsp

Instructions

  1.  
  1. Combine all ingredients together and mix well to ensure no lumps form.
  2. Microwave on high for 1.5 minutes.
  3. Serve with ice cream and blueberries or choco-chips as desired. Enjoy!

Notes

Serving suggestion Best enjoyed warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream or a drizzle of chocolate sauce.
Storage — Meant to be enjoyed fresh, but you can refrigerate leftovers for a few hours and reheat before serving.
Tip — For extra flavor, add a dash of vanilla extract or lemon zest to the batter.

title: Banana Bread Cookies
excerptHtml: Soft, naturally sweet, and studded with chocolate chips, these banana bread cookies are the perfect snack or tea-time treat. Moist,...
contentHtml:
Soft, naturally sweet, and studded with chocolate chips, these banana bread cookies are the perfect snack or tea-time treat. Moist, flavorful, and incredibly easy to make!

About the Recipe

Think of these as the perfect cross between banana bread and cookies — chewy on the inside, slightly crisp on the edges, and bursting with cozy banana flavor. A quick bake that turns overripe bananas into pure comfort!

Ingredients

  • Banana – 1
  • Olive oil – 30 ml
  • Brown sugar – 50 grams
  • Milk – 40 ml
  • Flour – 180 grams
  • Ground almonds – 25 grams
  • Baking soda – 1/4 tsp
  • Choco-chips – 20–30 grams

Instructions

  1. Mash the banana and mix with olive oil and brown sugar. Mix well.
  2. Add refined flour, ground almonds, and baking soda.
  3. Add milk little by little as needed to form a soft dough.
  4. Fold in the choco-chips.
  5. Make small balls of the dough and place on parchment paper or a silicon mat.
  6. Bake at 160°C for 10–12 minutes until the edges are darker.
  7. Store in an airtight container for up to 1 week.

Notes

title: 3 Ingredient Healthy Icecream
excerptHtml: 3 Ingredient Healthy Ice-cream A simple, guilt-free dessert made with just bananas and mangoes — creamy, naturally sweet, and...
contentHtml:
3 Ingredient Healthy Ice-cream


A simple, guilt-free dessert made with just bananas and mangoes — creamy, naturally sweet, and perfect for all ages.

About the Recipe

A quick blend of frozen bananas and mangoes transforms into a dreamy, velvety ice cream — no sugar, no guilt, and no ice cream maker needed! It’s the kind of treat that feels indulgent but is actually packed with goodness — ideal for summer cravings or post-meal dessert fixes.

Ingredients

  • Bananas – 2 pieces
  • Mangoes – 2 pieces
  • Milk – 3–4 teaspoons (adjust for desired creaminess)

Instructions

  1. Slice up the bananas and spread them in a single layer on a baking tray.
  2. Repeat the same for the mangoes.
  3. Freeze the trays for a minimum of 3 hours.
  4. Once the fruits are completely frozen, transfer them to a mixer grinder, along with 1–2 tablespoons of milk as needed, and churn till smooth.
  5. Your healthy mango ice cream is now ready to serve.

Notes

Serving suggestion – Top with chopped nuts, toasted coconut, or a drizzle of honey for extra flavor.
Storage – Store in an airtight container in the freezer for up to 2 weeks.
Tip – The riper the bananas, the sweeter your ice cream will be — no added sugar needed!

title: Tender Coconut Mousse
excerptHtml: Tender Coconut Mousse A light and creamy dessert infused with tender coconut and white chocolate — perfect for a refreshing,...
contentHtml:
Tender Coconut Mousse

A light and creamy dessert infused with tender coconut and white chocolate — perfect for a refreshing, tropical treat.

Recipe- Tender Coconut Mousse

Ingredients

  • 200 g Heavy Cream
  • 50 g Icing Sugar
  • 100 g Melted White Chocolate
  • 50 g Coconut Milk Powder
  • 100 g Tender Coconut Pieces

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, combine cream, icing sugar, melted white chocolate, and coconut milk powder.
  2. Whip until soft peaks form.
  3. Chop tender coconut pieces and fold into the mousse.
  4. Set into glasses or individual serving cups.
  5. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.
  6. Garnish with berries and serve chilled.

title: Royal falooda trifle pudding
excerptHtml: Royal falooda trifle pudding A decadent, multi-layered dessert combining soft vanilla sponge, fragrant rose custard, rose jelly, and falooda —...
contentHtml:
Royal falooda trifle pudding

A decadent, multi-layered dessert combining soft vanilla sponge, fragrant rose custard, rose jelly, and falooda — perfect for festive occasions or indulgent treats.

Recipe- Royal falooda trifle pudding

Ingredients

For Eggless Vanilla Sponge Cake-

  • 240 g Yogurt
  • 150 g Castor Sugar
  • 120 ml Oil
  • 1.5 tsp Vanilla Essence
  • 210 g Refined Flour
  • 1 + ¼ tsp Baking Powder
  • ¼ tsp Baking Soda

For Rose Custard-

  • 180 ml Milk (30% fat)
  • 80 g Amul Cream
  • 40 g Castor Sugar
  • 20 g Corn Flour
  • 60 ml Rose Syrup

For Rose Jelly-

  • 250 ml Water
  • 120 g Granulated Sugar
  • 60 g Rose Syrup
  • 2 tsp Agar Agar Powder
  • 1 tsp Lemon Juice
  • 1 tbsp Red Food Coloring (optional)

Instructions

Vanilla Sponge Cake

  1. Preheat oven to 160°C and whisk together yogurt, oil, sugar, and vanilla essence.
  2. Add dry ingredients and mix until a smooth batter forms.
  3. Pour into a lined or greased 7-inch square tin.
  4. Bake for 30 minutes or until a skewer inserted comes out clean.
  5. Cool completely and cut into small cubes.

Rose Custard

  1. Combine milk, cream, sugar, cornflour, and rose syrup in a pan.
  2. Whisk until the mixture comes to a boil.
  3. Cook for 1 minute, whisking constantly, until thickened. Remove from heat.
  4. Add boiled falooda sev and mix (boil falooda sev according to package instructions before adding to custard).

Rose Jelly

  1. Combine water, sugar, and rose syrup in a saucepan.
  2. Heat over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves.
  3. Bring mixture to a boil.
  4. Reduce heat to low, add agar agar powder, and stir constantly for 2–3 minutes.
  5. Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice.
  6. Pour mixture into a shallow tray or mold.
  7. Allow to cool and set at room temperature for 30–40 minutes. Once set, cut into desired shapes or cubes.

Assembly

  1. Layer vanilla cake cubes in a glass or bowl.
  2. Soak the cake with thinned rose syrup.
  3. Add a layer of basil seeds.
  4. Add a layer of rose jelly.
  5. Fill with prepared rose custard.
  6. Chill in the refrigerator for 30 minutes.
  7. Garnish with roasted nuts and rose petals and serve chilled.

title: Paan & Gulkand Truffles
excerptHtml: Paan & Gulkand Truffles A refreshing twist on traditional truffles infused with paan and gulkhand, offering a unique blend of...
contentHtml:
Paan & Gulkand Truffles

A refreshing twist on traditional truffles infused with paan and gulkhand, offering a unique blend of creamy, aromatic, and indulgent flavors.

Recipe- Paan & Gulkand Truffles

Ingredients

  • 250+100 grams White Chocolate ( 100 g for coating)
  • 100 grams Fresh Cream
  • 4 Paan (betel) Leaves
  • 80 grams Gulkand
  • 50 grams Desiccated Coconut

Instructions

  1. Heat cream with chopped paan leaves; simmer for 1–2 minutes. Cover and steep for 10–15 minutes.
  2. Melt white chocolate. Strain the infused cream into the melted chocolate and whisk until smooth.
  3. Chill the ganache for 3–4 hours or overnight.
  4. Make 5 g gulkand balls and freeze to ease filling.
  5. Scoop 15 g ganache portions, flatten, place a gulkand ball in the center, and seal. Chill for 10 minutes.
  6. Melt the coating chocolate and place coconut in a shallow bowl.
  7. Dip truffles in coating, let excess drip, then roll in coconut.
  8. Chill for 5–10 minutes to set.

title: Orange Gulab Jamun Posset
excerptHtml: Orange Gulab Jamun Posset A zesty and creamy dessert combining orange-flavored posset with mini gulab jamuns, served in fresh orange...
contentHtml:
Orange Gulab Jamun Posset

A zesty and creamy dessert combining orange-flavored posset with mini gulab jamuns, served in fresh orange cups. Refreshing, indulgent, and perfect chilled.

Recipe- Orange Gulab Jamun Posset

Ingredients

  • 400 ml 30% fat Amul Cream
  • 70 grams Castor Sugar
  • 60 ml Orange juice
  • Zest of 1 Orange (1 tbsp)
  • ½ tsp Corn Flour
  • 6 tbsp Mini Gulab Jamun

Instructions

  1. Cut oranges into halves and scoop out pulp to make cups.
  2. In a pan, combine cream, sugar, orange juice, zest, and corn flour.
  3. Mix and bring to a boil.
  4. Cook for 1 minute, until mixture thickens.
  5. Place a mini gulab jamun in each orange cup.
  6. Pour prepared mixture over gulab jamun.
  7. Refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours to set.
  8. Garnish with pistachio powder and serve chilled.

title: Old school Eggless Cola Cake
excerptHtml: Old school Eggless Cola Cake A nostalgic, soft, and fudgy eggless cake with a hint of cola — sweet, moist,...
contentHtml:
Old school Eggless Cola Cake

A nostalgic, soft, and fudgy eggless cake with a hint of cola — sweet, moist, and irresistibly old-school!

Recipe- Old school Eggless Cola Cake

Ingredients

  • Butter – 113 grams (1⁄2 cup)
  • Condensed milk – 200 grams (2/3 cup)
  • Castor sugar – 15 grams (1 tbsp)
  • Cocoa powder – 20 grams (1⁄4 cup)
  • Refined flour – 140 grams (1 cup)
  • Baking powder – 3⁄4 tsp
  • Baking soda – 1⁄4 tsp
  • Coca-Cola (or any other aerated drink of your choice!) – 150-200 ml, as needed
  • Pan size – 6 inch
  • Baking temperature- 170°C
  • Baking time – 30-35 mins

Instructions

  1. I began by beating together butter with condensed milk and a little sugar.
  2. Next, I added flour, baking powder, and baking soda, and finally added enough cola to adjust the consistency of the batter.
  3. Finally, I transferred the batter to a pan and beat it till a skewer came out clear.
  4. I dusted the cake with some icing sugar and yes, it turned out as good as I remembered it. You have to try this cake out and tell me what.

title: Oatmeal Chocolate Cookies
excerptHtml: Oatmeal Chocolate Cookies Crunchy, chocolatey cookies loaded with oats and chocolate chips — a perfect bite-sized treat for any time...
contentHtml:
Oatmeal Chocolate Cookies

Crunchy, chocolatey cookies loaded with oats and chocolate chips — a perfect bite-sized treat for any time of day.

Recipe- Oatmeal Chocolate Cookies

  • Ingredients
  • 20 g Butter
  • 35 g Castor Sugar
  • ¼ tsp Vanilla
  • 18 g Refined Flour
  • 3 g Cocoa Powder
  • 40 g Instant Oats
  • 1/8 tsp Baking Soda
  • 15 g Milk
  • 25 g Chocolate Chips

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, whisk together butter and sugar and add vanilla extract.
  2. Add oats, refined flour, cocoa powder, and baking soda and mix everything together.
  3. Add milk and adjust the quantity to form a smooth dough.
  4. Fold the chocolate chips into the dough.
  5. Roll the cookies into 25 g each and chill for 5–10 minutes.
  6. Bake at 160°C for 10 minutes.
  7. Once the cookies are cooled, dip half of the cookie in melted dark chocolate (optional).

title: Nutella Cookies Recipe
excerptHtml: Nutella Cookies Recipe Indulgent Nutella cookies with a gooey chocolate center, topped with hazelnuts for the perfect nutty crunch. Soft,...
contentHtml:
Nutella Cookies Recipe

Indulgent Nutella cookies with a gooey chocolate center, topped with hazelnuts for the perfect nutty crunch. Soft, rich, and irresistible in every bite!

Recipe- Nutella Cookies Recipe

Ingredients

  • Salted butter – 25 grams
  • Castor sugar – 30 grams
  • Vanilla essence – 1⁄4 tsp
  • Nutella – 50 grams
  • Refined flour – 105 grams
  • Baking soda – 1/8 tsp
  • Milk – 10-20 ml
  • Chopped Hazelnuts – 20 grams

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, whisk together butter and castor sugar.
  2. Add vanilla essence and Nutella and mix it well.
  3. Add the flour and baking soda.
  4. Add milk accordingly to form a smooth dough.
  5. Roll the cookies into 25 grams each and add a big piece of chocolate in center (for melting effect) and chill for 5-10 minutes.
  6. Bake at 160°C for 15 minutes.
  7. Once the cookies are chilled, drizzle some Nutella on top and add some chopped hazelnuts.

title: Motichoor Mava Cake
excerptHtml: Motichoor Mava Cake A rich and festive cake infused with the flavors of mawa, cardamom, and motichoor ladoos. Soft, aromatic,...
contentHtml:
Motichoor Mava Cake

A rich and festive cake infused with the flavors of mawa, cardamom, and motichoor ladoos. Soft, aromatic, and perfect for celebrations or as a royal tea-time treat

Recipe – Motichoor Mava Cake

Ingredients

  • 210 grams Refined Flour
  • 1⁄2 tsp + 1⁄4 tsp Baking Powder
  • 1⁄4 tsp + 1⁄8 tsp Baking Soda
  • 1⁄2 tsp Cardamom Powder
  • 90 grams Yogurt
  • 90 ml Oil
  • 150 grams Castor Sugar
  • 120 ml Milk
  • 120 grams Mawa (Khoya)

Instructions

  1. In a large mixing bowl, combine yogurt, sugar, and oil. Mix well.
  2. Sift in the dry ingredients: flour, baking powder, baking soda, and cardamom powder. Mix until just combined.
  3. Gradually add milk, mixing simultaneously.
  4. Crumble the mawa and add to the batter. Mix well.
  5. Crush 3 motichoor ladoos and fold into the batter.
  6. Transfer the batter to a piping bag.
  7. Pipe into lined molds.
  8. Top with chopped pistachios and crushed motichoor ladoos.
  9. Bake at 160°C (320°F) for 30 minutes or until a skewer inserted comes out clean.
  10. Allow to cool completely before demolding.
  11. Garnish with additional chopped pistachios.

Tips

  • Use high-quality mawa for best flavor.
  • Adjust baking time according to mold size.
  • Don’t overmix the batter.
  • Motichoor ladoos can be adjusted to desired taste.

title: Mint and Lemon Cookie
excerptHtml: Mint and Lemon Cookie Refreshing cookies with a zesty lemon flavor and cool mint notes, topped with a sweet tangy...
contentHtml:
Mint and Lemon Cookie

Refreshing cookies with a zesty lemon flavor and cool mint notes, topped with a sweet tangy lemon glaze. Perfectly crisp and aromatic!

Recipe – Mint and Lemon Cookie

Ingredients

For Cookies-

  • Salted butter – 113 grams
  • Powdered sugar – 50 grams
  • Mint leaves – 1 tbsp (finely chopped)
  • Lemon zest – 1⁄4 tsp
  • Castor sugar before adding – 10 grams
  • Refined Flour – 115 grams
  • Lemon juice – 10 ml

For Lemon Glaze-

  • Icing sugar – 100 grams (sifted)
  • Lemon juice – 15-20 ml

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, add finely chopped mint leaves and lemon zest and rub it with 10 grams of castor sugar and keep it aside.
  2. In another bowl, whisk together butter and sugar.
  3. Then add mint leaves and sugar mixture to the bowl.
  4. Add lemon juice to form a soft dough.
  5. Roll into a log and freeze it for minimum 30 minutes.
  6. Cut and bake at 160°C for 13 minutes as shown in the video.
  7. For the glaze, take 100 grams sifted icing sugar, add 15-20 ml of lemon juice, whisk together, pour into a piping bag, and drizzle on the baked cookies.

title: Mango Thumbprint Cookies
excerptHtml: Mango Thumbprint Cookies Buttery cookies with a soft center filled with sweet mango jam — crisp on the edges and...
contentHtml:
Mango Thumbprint Cookies

Buttery cookies with a soft center filled with sweet mango jam — crisp on the edges and fruity in every bite!

Recipe- Mango Thumbprint Cookies

Ingredients (for the cookies)

  • Butter – 56 grams (1⁄4 cup)
  • Castor sugar – 35 grams (3 tbsp)
  • Mango pulp – 1 tbsp
  • All purpose flour – 105 grams (3⁄4 cup)
  • Cornflour – 10 grams (1 tbsp)

Baking Details

  • Temperature – 170°C
  • Time – 16-18 mins

Instructions

  1. Beat together butter and icing sugar in a bowl till the butter is light and airy.
  2. Add fresh mango pulp and beat for a minute or two.
  3. Add the flour and combine everything to bring it together into a soft dough.
  4. If the dough feels too soft, refrigerate for 15 minutes. Otherwise, measure out small balls and place them on a baking tray leaving some distance between each.
  5. Use your thumb or the back of a small spoon to make an impression in the middle of the dough.
  6. Bake the cookies till the edges are golden brown.
  7. Allow the cookies to cool completely, then fill them with mango jam.
  8. Enjoy your mango thumbprint cookies and store them in an airtight container to keep them crisp and fresh.

title: Eggless Mango swirl cookies
excerptHtml: Eggless Mango swirl cookies The cutest Mango cookies on the internet. These are easy to make and pack a punch...
contentHtml:
Eggless Mango swirl cookies

The cutest Mango cookies on the internet. These are easy to make and pack a punch of the fresh tropical flavor of mangoes.

These mango cookies are one of my favourite mango-based recipes to try in the summers. It’s a hit in the family and an all-time favourite of my son which means they are polished off before I know it. These cookies are crisp, soft, and full of the tropical flavor of mangoes in every bite.

Recipe – Eggless Mango Swirl Cookies

Ingredients

  • Butter- 3 tbsp (42g)
  • Icing Sugar- ¾ cup (90g)
  • Refined Flour- ½ cup
  • Mango puree- 30 g
  • Corn flour- 2tsp

Method

  1. Preheat the oven to 160 degree C.
  2. In a bowl, add softened butter and sifted icing sugar. Beat the mixture well until its light and fluffy.
  3. Add the mango puree and once again whisk the mixture for a few seconds till it combines well.
  4. Sift the flour and corn flour and using a spatula mix everything together till you have a soft and smooth dough.
  5. Fill the dough in a piping bag fitted with a star nozzle.
  6. Pipe out rosettes on a baking tray lined with parchment paper or silicon mat.
  7. Bake in a preheated oven at 160 degrees C for 10 to 15 mins or until the edges and the bottom of the cookie are slightly darker.
  8. The cookies will crisp up once they cool completely. Store these cookies in an air tight container for upto 10 days.

title: Mango Iced Tea
excerptHtml: Mango Iced Tea A refreshing summer cooler that combines the goodness of tea and mangoes – perfect for beating the...
contentHtml:
Mango Iced Tea

A refreshing summer cooler that combines the goodness of tea and mangoes – perfect for beating the heat and enjoying on repeat!

Recipe- Mango Iced Tea

Ingredients

  • Brewed tea – as strong or mild as you like
  • Mango pulp – 1-2 tbsp
  • Lemon juice – as desired
  • Mint leaves – as desired
  • Ice – lots

Instructions

  1. Brew some tea — as strong or mild as you like it.
  2. Strain and allow it to cool.
  3. In a glass, add 1-2 tbsp of mango pulp, lemon juice, mint leaves, lots of ice, and the brewed tea.
  4. Shake or mix well and enjoy!

title: Mango Colada Smoothie Bowl
excerptHtml: Mango Colada Smoothie Bowl A creamy, tropical smoothie bowl made with mango, banana, pineapple, and coconut milk — a sunshine-filled...
contentHtml:
Mango Colada Smoothie Bowl

A creamy, tropical smoothie bowl made with mango, banana, pineapple, and coconut milk — a sunshine-filled breakfast that tastes like summer in every spoonful!

 

About the Recipe

This mango colada smoothie bowl is one of my favourite ways to start a hot day. It’s naturally sweet, loaded with tropical flavours, and feels like dessert but is actually a wholesome, filling meal. I first made this during mango season when I had leftover fruit in the freezer — and it’s been on repeat ever since! Perfect for anyone who loves quick, nutritious, egg-free breakfast ideas.

Ingredients

  • Frozen banana – ½ cup

  • Mango chunks – ½ cup

  • Pineapple chunks – 1 cup

  • Coconut milk – ¾ cup to 1 cup (adjust for desired consistency)

    Fruits can be used fresh or frozen, but if using fresh, cut and freeze them for 2–3 hours for best consistency.

Instructions

  1. Add the banana, mango, and pineapple to a blender jar.
  2. Pour in the coconut milk and blend until smooth and creamy.
  3. For a smoothie bowl, keep it thicker to hold the toppings. Add more milk if you prefer a thinner smoothie.
  4. Pour into a bowl and top with kiwi slices, fresh mango, granola, and chia seeds.
  5. Enjoy immediately or refrigerate until ready to serve.

Notes & Tips

  • Frozen fruit makes the smoothie thick and creamy. If your fruit isn’t frozen, add a few ice cubes while blending.

  • The banana and mango add natural sweetness — add honey or sugar only if you prefer it sweeter.

  • Get creative with toppings — try nut butters, seeds, or toasted coconut flakes.

  • Best served fresh, but can be refrigerated for up to 24 hours.

  • This bowl is nutrient-dense and filling — perfect as a complete meal, not a light snack.

  1.

title: Mango Fruit Cream
excerptHtml: Mango Fruit Cream A luscious, fruity dessert layered with soft vanilla sponge, whipped cream, and juicy mangoes — perfectly chilled...
contentHtml:
Mango Fruit Cream

A luscious, fruity dessert layered with soft vanilla sponge, whipped cream, and juicy mangoes — perfectly chilled for warm days or festive gatherings.

 

Ingredients

Vanilla sponge – 1 cup
Whipped cream – 2 cups
Condensed milk – ¼ cup
Mangoes – 2–3 pieces
Mango pulp – ¼ cup

You can use store-bought or homemade sponge. If mangoes aren’t in season, use canned pulp for the same rich flavor.

Instructions

  1. Whip the cream until soft peaks form and gently fold in condensed milk.

  2. In your serving bowl, layer pieces of vanilla sponge.

  3. Add a layer of fresh mango pieces followed by the whipped-cream mixture.

  4. Spoon some mango pulp on top for an extra burst of flavor.

  5. Repeat the layers, finishing with whipped cream and mangoes on top.

  6. Chill for at least an hour before serving.

Notes & Tips

  • Make-ahead friendly: Prepare and refrigerate up to 24 hours before serving.

  • Add crushed nuts or toasted coconut for texture.

  • Substitute mangoes with strawberries or mixed fruits when not in season.

  • Serve in individual glasses for a neat, festive presentation.

  • Best enjoyed cold — the cream firms up beautifully and the flavors meld together.

title: Kitkat Cookies
excerptHtml: Kitkat Cookies Soft, chewy cookies packed with chunks of KitKat and dark chocolate—perfectly crisp on the edges and gooey in...
contentHtml:
Kitkat Cookies

Soft, chewy cookies packed with chunks of KitKat and dark chocolate—perfectly crisp on the edges and gooey in the center. A must-try treat for all chocolate lovers!

Recipe- Kitkat Cookies

Ingredients

  • 75 grams Butter
  • 50 grams Castor Sugar
  • 50 grams Brown Sugar
  • 1⁄2 tsp Vanilla
  • 140 grams Flour
  • 1⁄2 tsp Baking Powder (BP)
  • 1⁄4 tsp Baking Soda (BS)
  • 45 ml Milk
  • 80 grams KitKat (chopped)
  • 65 grams Dark Callets

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, combine butter and both sugars and whisk together. Add vanilla and mix well.
  2. Add flour, baking powder, and baking soda.
  3. Add milk to form a smooth dough.
  4. Fold chopped KitKat and dark callets into the dough.
  5. Roll the cookies into 50 grams each and chill for 15 minutes.
  6. Bake at 160°C for 15 minutes.

title: Kaju Katli Cookies
excerptHtml: Kaju Katli Cookies Buttery, nutty cookies inspired by the classic Indian sweet kaju katli, with a delicate crunch and a...
contentHtml:
Kaju Katli Cookies

Buttery, nutty cookies inspired by the classic Indian sweet kaju katli, with a delicate crunch and a luxurious silver varak finish.

Recipe- Kaju Katli Cookies

Ingredients

  • 70 g Refined Flour
  • 40 g Cashew Powder
  • 35 g Salted Butter (cubed)
  • ¼ tsp Cardamom Powder
  • 50–60 g Condensed Milk
  • 1 tbsp Silver Varak (for garnish)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 160°C (320°F).
  2. Grind cashews into a fine powder.
  3. In a large bowl, combine flour, cashew powder, and cardamom powder.
  4. Add cold cubed butter and rub until mixture resembles sand.
  5. Gradually add condensed milk, mixing until a soft dough forms.
  6. Roll out dough between two mats or parchment papers to desired thickness.
  7. Cut into desired shapes using a knife or a cookie cutter.
  8. Bake for 15 minutes or until edges turn golden brown.
  9. Allow cookies to cool until warm.
  10. Garnish with silver varak for a luxurious look.

title: Jam Drop Cookies
excerptHtml: Jam Drop Cookies Soft buttery cookies with a sweet jam center — perfect for tea time or as a delightful...
contentHtml:
Jam Drop Cookies

Soft buttery cookies with a sweet jam center — perfect for tea time or as a delightful treat for any occasion.

Recipe – Jam Drop Cookies

Ingredients

  • Butter – 56 grams
  • Castor sugar – 35 grams
  • Vanilla essence – 1⁄2 tsp
  • Flour – 70 grams
  • Cornflour – 4 grams
  • Jam of choice – 50 grams

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, whisk together butter, sugar, and vanilla essence.
  2. Add flour and cornflour and mix it well to form a soft dough.
  3. Roll the cookies into gram-sized balls each and flatten them.
  4. Make an indent in the center of each cookie using a small spoon or your thumb.
  5. Bake at 160°C for 15 minutes.
  6. Once the cookies are cooled completely, fill the indents with jam of your choice.

title: Instant Thandai Masala Powder
excerptHtml: Instant Thandai Masala Powder A fragrant and flavorful blend of nuts, seeds, and spices that instantly transforms milk into the...
contentHtml:
Instant Thandai Masala Powder

A fragrant and flavorful blend of nuts, seeds, and spices that instantly transforms milk into the classic Indian Thandai drink — perfect for warm or chilled servings.

Recipe-Instant Thandai Masala Powder

Ingredients

  • Almonds – 50 grams
  • Cashews – 50 grams
  • Pistachios – 30 grams
  • Whole Peppercorns – 1 tsp
  • Fennel Seeds – 1 tbsp
  • Poppy Seeds – 1 tbsp
  • Melon Seeds – 1 tbsp
  • Cardamom Pods – 10-12
  • Edible Rose Petals – 1 tbsp
  • Saffron – few strands

Instructions

  1. Add all the ingredients to a spice grinder or a mixer.
  2. Grind till you have a fine powder.
  3. Transfer to an airtight container.
  4. To make thandai milk, add 2-3 heaping tbsp of powder to a cup of warm milk and sweetener of choice.
  5. Serve warm or refrigerate and serve chilled.

title: Homemade Condensed Milk
excerptHtml: Homemade Condensed Milk Rich and creamy homemade condensed milk made with just milk and sugar. Perfect for desserts, beverages, and...
contentHtml:
Homemade Condensed Milk

Rich and creamy homemade condensed milk made with just milk and sugar. Perfect for desserts, beverages, and baking needs.

Recipe- Homemade Condensed Milk

Ingredients

  • 500 ml Milk
  • 200 grams Sugar
  • ¼ tsp Baking Soda

Instructions

  1. In a saucepan, combine milk and sugar.
  2. Heat over medium, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture begins to thicken.
  3. As the mixture heats, avoid stirring in any scum that forms on the surface.
  4. Continue stirring constantly over medium heat until the mixture thickens and reaches a boil.
  5. Add baking soda and stir gently until the froth settles.
  6. Allow the condensed milk to cool completely, then transfer it to a jar for storage.

title: Healthier oats and Carrot cake
excerptHtml: Healthier oats and Carrot cake A wholesome and moist cake made with oats, carrots, and bananas—naturally sweet and perfect for...
contentHtml:
Healthier oats and Carrot cake

A wholesome and moist cake made with oats, carrots, and bananas—naturally sweet and perfect for a guilt-free treat. Enjoy it as is or with a simple cream cheese glaze.

Recipe – Healthier Oats and Carrot Cake

Ingredients

  • 120 ml Milk
  • 20 grams Castor Sugar
  • 45 ml Oil
  • 50 grams Oats Flour
  • 50 grams Rolled Oats
  • 2 tbsp Medium ripe bananas
  • 70 grams Carrot (grated)
  • 1 tsp Cinnamon
  • ½ tsp Baking Powder
  • 35 grams Walnuts

Instructions

  1. For this cake, use rolled oats as well as oats flour, made at home by grinding instant oats in a blender.
  2. Add cinnamon powder, sugar, baking powder, grated carrots, and oil.
  3. Mash two over-ripe bananas and add them to the mixture.
  4. Add milk to adjust the consistency of the batter and fold in walnuts.
  5. The batter may not look great at this point, but it comes together nicely.
  6. Bake until a skewer comes out clean.
  7. Top with your favorite frosting or enjoy as is; an easy cream cheese glaze with mini sugar carrots works beautifully.
  8. Cut a slice and enjoy!

title: Healthier Mocha Oats Cake
excerptHtml: Healthier Mocha Oats Cake A wholesome cake made with oats, ragi, and a hint of coffee — fudgy, chocolaty, and...
contentHtml:
Healthier Mocha Oats Cake

A wholesome cake made with oats, ragi, and a hint of coffee — fudgy, chocolaty, and perfect for a healthier treat!

Recipe-Healthier Mocha Oats Cake

Ingredients

  • Oil – 45 ml (3 tbsp)
  • Milk – 100-120 ml (1⁄2 cup)
  • Castor sugar – 65 grams (1⁄3 cup)
  • Coffee powder – 2 tsp
  • Oats flour – 80 grams
  • Ragi flour – 20 grams
  • Baking powder – 3⁄4 tsp
  • Cocoa powder – 10 grams (1 tbsp)
  • Chocolate chips – as needed

Instructions

  1. Add instant coffee powder to warm milk and allow the coffee to dissolve.
  2. Add sugar and oil and whisk everything well.
  3. Add oats flour. You can make this at home by grinding instant oats into a fine powder.
  4. Add ragi flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and combine everything.
  5. Add chocolate chips for good measure.
  6. Bake until a skewer comes out clean.
  7. Optionally, top the cake with melted chocolate mixed with chopped roasted almonds.
  8. Cut yourself a slice and enjoy!

Pan size – 6 inch

  • Baking temperature – 160°C
  • Baking time – 25-30 mins

title: Hazelnut Nutella Loaf Cake
excerptHtml: Hazelnut Nutella Loaf Cake A rich and moist hazelnut Nutella loaf cake swirled with creamy Nutella and topped with crunchy...
contentHtml:
Hazelnut Nutella Loaf Cake

A rich and moist hazelnut Nutella loaf cake swirled with creamy Nutella and topped with crunchy roasted hazelnuts—pure indulgence in every slice.

Recipe- Hazelnut Nutella Loaf Cake

Ingredients

  • 180 grams Yogurt
  • 150 grams Castor Sugar
  • 120 ml Oil
  • 140 grams Refined Flour
  • 35 grams Cocoa Powder
  • 1 tsp Baking Powder
  • ¼ tsp Baking Soda
  • 80 ml Milk
  • ¼ cup Roasted Hazelnuts
  • 100 grams Nutella (50g inside batter + 50g on top)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 170°C.
  2. In a big bowl, whisk together yogurt, castor sugar, and oil.
  3. Add flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and milk to combine a smooth batter.
  4. Fold in some Nutella and chopped roasted hazelnuts.
  5. Transfer the batter into a greased lined pan and top with more Nutella swirls and chopped hazelnuts.
  6. Bake for 40-45 minutes, until a skewer comes out clean.
  7.

title: Gluten Free Chocolate & Oats Cake
excerptHtml: Gluten Free Chocolate & Oats Cake A quick and indulgent chocolate oats cake made in a blender—rich, fudgy, and loaded...
contentHtml:
Gluten Free Chocolate & Oats Cake

A quick and indulgent chocolate oats cake made in a blender—rich, fudgy, and loaded with chocolate chips for that perfect bite.

Recipe- Gluten Free Chocolate & Oats Cake

Ingredients

  • 120 grams Instant Oats
  • 1 tsp Baking Powder
  • 40 grams Cocoa Powder
  • 1 tsp Coffee
  • 3 tbsp Castor Sugar
  • 240 ml Milk
  • Chocolate Chips (as needed)
  • Chocolate Chunks (as needed)

Instructions

  1. Blend everything together in a mixer blender.
  2. Add oats, cocoa powder, sugar, vanilla, instant coffee, baking powder, and milk.
  3. Transfer the batter to the pan and top it up with some chocolate chunks and chocolate chips.
  4. Bake at 180°C until done

title: Fresh Mango Jam
excerptHtml: Fresh Mango Jam A simple and quick three-ingredient jam made with ripe mangoes, sugar, and lemon juice — perfect on...
contentHtml:
Fresh Mango Jam

A simple and quick three-ingredient jam made with ripe mangoes, sugar, and lemon juice — perfect on toast, cakes, or tarts!

Recipe – Fresh Mango Jam

Ingredients

  • Mango diced – 3 cups
  • Sugar – 50 grams
  • Lemon juice – 2 tsp

Instructions

  1. Place freshly chopped mango cubes along with sugar and lemon juice in a pan.
  2. Cook the mangoes while stirring continuously and smashing them as they soften.
  3. Initially, the mangoes will release a lot of water, but continue stirring until it thickens.
  4. Test if the jam is ready by placing a spoonful on a plate; if it doesn’t slide down immediately, the jam is ready.
  5. Allow the jam to cool, then transfer it to a sterilized glass jar and keep it refrigerated.
  6. Enjoy the jam over toast, cakes, tarts, or anything else you like.

title: Espresso Bean Cookies
excerptHtml: Espresso Bean Cookies Crispy, chocolatey cookies shaped like coffee beans with a rich espresso flavor in every bite. Perfect for...
contentHtml:
Espresso Bean Cookies

Crispy, chocolatey cookies shaped like coffee beans with a rich espresso flavor in every bite. Perfect for coffee lovers as a snack or dessert accompaniment.

Recipe- Espresso Bean Cookies

Ingredients

  • Flour- 70 grams
  • Butter- 60 grams
  • Cocoa powder- 12 grams
  • Icing sugar- 30 grams
  • Water- 20 grams
  • Coffee powder- 4 grams

Instructions

  1. In a bowl mix warm water and coffee powder and keep it aside.
  2. In another bowl cream together butter and icing sugar
  3. Add flour and cocoa powder
  4. Then add the Coffee decoction and fold everything to form a smooth dough.
  5. Measure small balls of dough and shape it like a coffee bean as shown in the video.
  6. Use a skewer to make an indent in the center of the dough.
  7. Bake at 160 degrees for 8 minutes

title: Eggless Semolina Cake
excerptHtml: Eggless Semolina Cake An old-school classic that will remind you of the Iyengar bakery style cake that you’re sure to...
contentHtml:
Eggless Semolina Cake

An old-school classic that will remind you of the Iyengar bakery style cake that you’re sure to have eaten at some point if you grew up in India.

Recipe – Semolina Cake

  • Fine Semolina (rava) – 1 + ¼ cup (200 g)
  • All-purpose flour – ¼ cup
  • Caster Sugar – ¾ cup
  • Baking Powder – 1 tsp
  • Baking Soda – ½ tsp
  • Oil – 1/3 cup
  • Yogurt – ½ cup
  • Milk – ½ cup – ¾ cup
  • Zest of one lemon (optional)

Method

  1. Line a 7-inch round pan and keep aside.
  2. In a bowl, mix the semolina, flour, sugar, oil, yogurt, ½ cup of milk and lemon zest and mix well. Cover and leave it for 15-20 minutes.
  3. Meanwhile, pre-heat the oven to 180 degree C.
  4. After 15 minutes, add the baking powder, and baking soda. Whisk the batter and if it’s too thick add the remaining ¼ cup of milk – a few tsp at a time to check for consistency. You don’t want a very thin batter.
  5. Pour it into the pan and bake for 30-35 minutes or until an inserted toothpick comes out clean.
  6. Let it cool slightly then remove from pan and transfer to a cooling rack. Cool completely before slicing.

title: Eggless Orange and Dark Chocolate Cake
excerptHtml: A moist, zesty cake infused with fresh orange flavor and rich dark chocolate chunks – a perfect balance of citrusy...
contentHtml:
A moist, zesty cake infused with fresh orange flavor and rich dark chocolate chunks – a perfect balance of citrusy freshness and indulgent sweetness.

Recipe- Eggless Orange and Dark Chocolate Cake

Ingredients

  • 100 g Castor Sugar
  • 3 tsp Orange Zest
  • 120 g Yogurt
  • 80 ml Oil
  • 120 ml Orange Juice
  • 140 g Refined Flour
  • 1 tsp Baking Powder
  • 1⁄4 tsp Baking Soda
  • 100 g Chopped Chocolate
  • 50 g Chocolate Chips

Instructions

  1. Chop some dark chocolate into large chunks.
  2. Zest an orange and rub the zest with sugar to release its oil and enhance the citrusy flavor.
  3. Whisk yogurt, followed by oil and freshly squeezed orange juice.
  4. Add flour, baking powder, and baking soda, then combine everything into a smooth batter.
  5. Add a handful of chocolate chips and chocolate chunks, folding them into the batter.
  6. Transfer the batter to a greased pan and top with more chocolate chips and chunks.
  7. Bake the cake until it’s golden brown or until a skewer comes out clean.
  8. Let the cake cool completely, then cut a slice to serve or store for up to 5 days

title: Eggless Oats, Chocolate & Orange Cake
excerptHtml: Eggless Oats, Chocolate & Orange Cake A healthy, egg-free chocolate and orange cake made with oats flour — fudgy, citrusy,...
contentHtml:
Eggless Oats, Chocolate & Orange Cake

A healthy, egg-free chocolate and orange cake made with oats flour — fudgy, citrusy, and perfect for a guilt-free treat!

Recipe- Eggless Oats, Chocolate & Orange Cake

Ingredients

  • Castor sugar – 75 grams
  • Orange zest – 1 tbsp
  • Milk – 130 ml
  • Orange juice – 30 ml
  • Oil – 40 ml
  • Oats powder – 120 grams
  • Cocoa powder – 15 grams
  • Baking powder – 1 tsp

Instructions

  1. Grind some instant oats into a fine powder and set it aside.
  2. Rub together sugar with orange zest.
  3. Add milk, orange juice, and oil and whisk everything together.
  4. Coarsen the oats powder along with cocoa powder and baking powder, and combine everything really well.
  5. Optionally, add chocolate chips if you like.
  6. Bake in a preheated oven at 170°C until a skewer comes out clean.
  7. Top with melted chocolate and garnish with fresh orange peels.
  8. Cut yourself a slice and enjoy!

title: Eggless Oats and Biscoff Cake
excerptHtml: Eggless Oats and Biscoff Cake A wholesome, moist, and flavorful cake made with oats and rich Biscoff spread — the...
contentHtml:
Eggless Oats and Biscoff Cake

A wholesome, moist, and flavorful cake made with oats and rich Biscoff spread — the perfect guilt-free indulgence for dessert lovers.

Recipe- Eggless Oats and Biscoff Cake

Ingredients

  • 60 g Yogurt
  • 120 ml Milk
  • 30 g Brown Sugar
  • 50 ml Oil
  • 1 tsp Vanilla Extract
  • 120 g Oats (ground into fine powder)
  • 1 tsp Baking Powder
  • 100 g Biscoff Spread

Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 170°C and prepare a 5-inch pan.
  2. Grind the instant oats into a fine powder and set aside.
  3. In a bowl, whisk together yogurt, milk, brown sugar, oil, and vanilla until smooth.
  4. Add the oats powder and baking powder, mixing until you get a smooth batter.
  5. Melt the Biscoff spread slightly and fold it into the batter.
  6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 22–25 minutes or until a skewer inserted comes out clean.
  7. Once baked, spread some additional Biscoff spread on top and garnish with a Biscoff biscuit.
  8. Slice and enjoy your delicious, eggless treat!

title: Eggless Mango Sorbets
excerptHtml: Eggless Mango Sorbets A refreshing, one-ingredient mango sorbet to beat the summer heat — creamy, naturally sweet, and perfect served...
contentHtml:
Eggless Mango Sorbets

A refreshing, one-ingredient mango sorbet to beat the summer heat — creamy, naturally sweet, and perfect served chilled with a hint of spice

Recipe – Eggless Mango Sorbets

Ingredients

  • Ripe mangoes – 3 whole

Instructions

  1. Cut mangoes into small cubes and spread them on a tray lined with parchment.
  2. Freeze for at least 3 hours.
  3. Transfer the frozen mango cubes to a mixer grinder and churn until smooth.
  4. Scoop out the sorbet and serve with a sprinkle of red chilli powder.
  5. Keep the sorbet frozen if not consuming immediately.

Tips

  • If the mangoes are not sweet enough, add 1-2 tbsp of sugar; avoid liquid sweeteners to prevent a thin sorbet.
  • Cut even pieces of mango for uniform freezing.
  • If your freezer develops frost, place a loose piece of cling film on top of the mango cubes.
  • Churn while the mangoes are still frozen. Do not allow them to soften.
  • Avoid over-churning, as the heat from the mixer can turn the sorbet into mango juice.

title: Eggless Mango Pound Cake
excerptHtml: Eggless Mango Pound Cake A soft, buttery pound cake bursting with tropical mango flavor – perfectly moist and egg-free, ideal...
contentHtml:
Eggless Mango Pound Cake

A soft, buttery pound cake bursting with tropical mango flavor – perfectly moist and egg-free, ideal for tea-time or dessert.

Recipe- Eggless Mango Pound Cake

Ingredients

  • 90 ml Milk
  • 10 ml Vinegar
  • 150 grams Castor Sugar
  • 80 grams Oil
  • 30 grams Yogurt
  • 1 tbsp Lemon Zest
  • 160 grams Mango Pulp
  • 190 grams Refined Flour
  • 25 grams Custard Powder
  • 1.5 tsp Baking Powder
  • ½ tsp Baking Soda

Instructions

  1. First, make the buttermilk by combining warm milk and vinegar.
  2. Next, add yogurt, sugar, and oil, and whisk everything together.
  3. Then, add lemon zest and mango pulp, and combine well.
  4. Finally, add the flour, custard powder, baking powder, and baking soda, and make a smooth batter.
  5. Pour the batter into a Bundt pan (you can also use a loaf pan or round baking pan).
  6. Bake at 180°C until a skewer inserted comes out clean.

title: Eggless Mango Madeleines
excerptHtml: Eggless Mango Madeleines Soft, buttery, and egg-free Madeleines infused with mango — perfect on their own or paired with a...
contentHtml:
Eggless Mango Madeleines

Soft, buttery, and egg-free Madeleines infused with mango — perfect on their own or paired with a cup of coffee or chai!

Recipe – Eggless Mango Madeleines

Ingredients

  • Yogurt – 60 grams (1⁄4 cup)
  • Castor sugar – 100 grams (1⁄2 cup)
  • Milk – 80 grams (1⁄3 cup)
  • Mango pulp – 40 grams (2.5 tbsp)
  • Refined flour – 140 grams (1 cup)
  • Baking powder – 3⁄4 tsp
  • Baking soda – 1⁄4 tsp
  • Melted butter – 56 grams (1⁄4 cup)

Instructions

  1. Combine yogurt, milk, and sugar in a bowl.
  2. Add mango pulp for flavor. Alfonso mango pulp is preferred, but any variety works.
  3. Add flour, baking powder, and baking soda, and combine well.
  4. Finally, add melted butter to the batter — this gives Madeleines their signature hum.
  5. Brush your Madeleine pan with butter and pipe out the batter into each cavity.
  6. Bake until a skewer comes out clean.
  7. Enjoy as is with a dusting of icing sugar, or dunk in chai or coffee!

title: Eggless Mango Biscuit Pudding
excerptHtml: Eggless Mango Biscuit Pudding A creamy, fruity dessert layering mango custard and milk-soaked biscuits — perfect served chilled with a...
contentHtml:
Eggless Mango Biscuit Pudding

A creamy, fruity dessert layering mango custard and milk-soaked biscuits — perfect served chilled with a topping of fresh mangoes or white chocolate!

Recipe – Eggless Mango Biscuit Pudding

Ingredients

For the Custard-

  • Milk – 1.5 cups (360 ml)
  • Castor sugar – 1⁄4 cup (50 grams)
  • Cornflour – 1⁄4 cup (40 grams)
  • Mango pulp – 6 tbsp (80 grams)

For the Mango Milk-

  • Warm milk – 100 ml
  • Mango pulp – 1 tbsp

Instructions

  1. First, make mango custard by combining milk, sugar, cornflour, and mango pulp and bring this to a rapid boil.
  2. Allow the mixture to cool.
  3. Meanwhile, prepare mango milk by simply adding a spoonful of mango pulp or puréed mangoes to warm milk.
  4. Use any biscuits of your choice; I have used Marie Gold.
  5. Dip each biscuit in the milk mixture and arrange them in a serving bowl. Don’t worry if the biscuits have a slight overlap.
  6. Pour the prepared custard over the biscuits.
  7. Repeat both the layers if your bowl is not as deep; you can make this in a single layer as well.
  8. Allow the custard to set in the refrigerator for a minimum of one hour.
  9. Once the custard has fully set, top it up with some fresh mangoes or serve it with whipped cream if you like.
  10. Optionally, chop some white chocolate to put over the top — this makes the custard look prettier and taste even better.
  11. Serve the pudding cold and enjoy.

title: Magic Eggless Fudgy Chocolate Almond Cake
excerptHtml: Magic Eggless Fudgy Chocolate Almond Cake This magic cake needs no leavening agents — yet turns out irresistibly fudgy, moist,...
contentHtml:
Magic Eggless Fudgy Chocolate Almond Cake

This magic cake needs no leavening agents — yet turns out irresistibly fudgy, moist, and nutty with rich chocolate and almond flavors. Pure indulgence in every bite!

Recipe- Magic Eggless Fudgy Chocolate Almond Cake

Ingredients

  • Melted butter – 100 grams
  • Melted chocolate – 90 grams
  • Powdered sugar – 100 grams
  • Refined flour – 105 grams
  • Almond flour or powdered almonds – 100 grams
  • Milk – 90 ml

Instructions

  1. Whisk together melted butter, melted dark chocolate, and powdered sugar
    until smooth.
  2. Add almond flour, refined flour, and milk to form a thick batter.
  3. Pour the batter into a 6-inch baking pan.
  4. Top with flaked almonds.
  5. Bake at 160°C for 35–40 minutes or until a skewer inserted comes out almost
    clean.
  6. Let it cool slightly before slicing. Enjoy the rich, fudgy texture and nutty
    almond flavor!

Pan Size

  • 6 inch

Baking Details

  • Temperature – 160°C
  • Time – 35–40 minutes

title: Eggless Double Chocolate Chunk Muffins
excerptHtml: Eggless Double Chocolate Chunk Muffins Rich, fudgy, and loaded with chocolate chunks — these eggless cookies are a perfect treat...
contentHtml:
Eggless Double Chocolate Chunk Muffins

Rich, fudgy, and loaded with chocolate chunks — these eggless cookies are a perfect treat for all chocolate lovers.

Recipe – Eggless Double Chocolate Chunk Muffins

Ingredients

  • 11⁄2 cups All-Purpose Flour
  • 1⁄4 cup Cocoa Powder
  • 1 tsp Baking Powder
  • 1⁄4 tsp Baking Soda
  • 1 cup Yogurt
  • 1⁄4 cup Oil
  • 3⁄4 cup Castor Sugar
  • 1⁄2 tsp Vanilla Extract
  • 1⁄2 cup Chocolate Chunks

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 190°C.
  2. Whisk together the yogurt, oil, sugar, and vanilla.
  3. Add the dry ingredients in batches and make a thick batter.
  4. Add 1–2 tsp milk to adjust batter consistency if needed.
  5. Fold the chocolate chunks into the batter.
  6. Fill muffin liners with the batter and top with more chocolate.
  7. Bake for 18–20 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.
  8. This recipe makes 10 muffins.

title: Eggless Brookies (Brownie Cookies)
excerptHtml: Eggless Brookies (Brownie Cookies) A perfect fusion of fudgy brownies and chewy cookies — rich, chocolatey, and completely eggless for...
contentHtml:
Eggless Brookies (Brownie Cookies)

A perfect fusion of fudgy brownies and chewy cookies — rich, chocolatey, and completely eggless for everyone to enjoy.

Ingredients

  • 42 g Butter (melted)
  • 50 g Castor Sugar
  • 70 g Refined Flour
  • 20 g Cocoa Powder
  • ¼ tsp Baking Powder
  • ¼ tsp Baking Soda
  • 30 ml Milk
  • ¼ cup Choco Chips

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 180°C.
  2. In a bowl, combine flour, cocoa powder, sugar, baking powder, and baking soda.
  3. Add melted butter and milk to the mixture and combine well.
  4. Add chocolate chips on top of each brookie before baking for a nicer look.
  5. Bake for 20–25 minutes.
  6. Allow the brookies to cool completely, then store them in an airtight container.

title: Eggless Bakery Style Rawa Cake
excerptHtml: Eggless Bakery Style Rawa Cake A soft, moist, and aromatic bakery-style rava cake made with semolina, yogurt, and a hint...
contentHtml:
Eggless Bakery Style Rawa Cake

A soft, moist, and aromatic bakery-style rava cake made with semolina, yogurt, and a hint of pineapple essence—perfect for tea-time indulgence

Recipe-Eggless Bakery Style Rawa Cake

Ingredients

  • 120 grams Yogurt
  • 180 ml Milk
  • 200 grams Semolina/Rava
  • 50 grams Ghee
  • 30 ml Oil
  • 1⁄4 tsp Pineapple Essence
  • 150 grams Castor Sugar
  • 35 grams Refined Flour
  • 1 tbsp Custard Powder
  • 1 tsp Baking Powder
  • 1⁄2 tsp Baking Soda

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 180°C.
  2. In a bowl, add yogurt, milk, and fine semolina/rava.
  3. Stir, cover, and set aside for 15 minutes.
  4. Once the mixture has thickened, add ghee, oil, essence, and sugar; mix well.
  5. Add flour, custard powder, baking powder, and baking soda; whisk to make a smooth batter.
  6. Transfer the batter to a greased pan and bake for 35-40 minutes or until a skewer comes out clean.
  7. Allow the cake to cool, then cut into slices and serve or store in an airtight container for up to 5 days.

title: Egg-Free One Bowl Mango Biscoff Cake
excerptHtml: Egg-Free One Bowl Mango Biscoff Cake A quick and easy one-bowl cake combining the tropical sweetness of mango with the...
contentHtml:
Egg-Free One Bowl Mango Biscoff Cake

A quick and easy one-bowl cake combining the tropical sweetness of mango with the caramel goodness of Biscoff — ready in just minutes and perfect for a single-serve treat.

Recipe – Egg-Free One Bowl Mango Biscoff Cake

Ingredients

  • Milk – 1/2 cup (120 grams)
  • Mango puree – 2 1/2 tbsp (40 grams)
  • Oil – 2 1/2 tbsp (40 ml)
  • Castor sugar – 1/3 cup (75 grams)
  • Flour – 1/2 cup (70 grams)
  • Baking powder – 1/2 tsp
  • Biscoff – 2 tbsp

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, whisk together milk, castor sugar, and oil.
  2. Add mango puree (store-bought or homemade) and whisk well.
  3. Mix in flour and baking powder until you get a smooth batter.
  4. Add spoonfuls of Biscoff into the batter; swirl or leave it in the middle as
    desired.
  5. Microwave the bowl on high for 1 1/2 to 2 minutes, or until a skewer comes
    out clean.
  6. Serve with additional chopped mangoes and Biscoff on top.
  7. For a plain mango version, skip the Biscoff.

title: Coconut Chocolate Cookies
excerptHtml: Coconut Chocolate Cookies Soft and chewy cookies with a rich coconut flavor, lightly sweetened and perfect for a quick indulgent...
contentHtml:
Coconut Chocolate Cookies

Soft and chewy cookies with a rich coconut flavor, lightly sweetened and perfect for a quick indulgent treat.

Recipe – Coconut Chocolate Cookies

Ingredients

  • 90 g Dessicated Coconut
  • 100 g Condensed Milk
  • 35 g Flour
  • ¼ tsp Baking Powder
  • 90–100 ml Milk

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, mix condensed milk and dessicated coconut.
  2. Add flour and baking powder.
  3. Add milk as needed to form a soft dough.
  4. Pipe the cookies on a silicon mat.
  5. Bake at 170°C for 8–10 minutes.
  6. Store in an airtight container.

title: Coconut & Mango Tea cake
excerptHtml: Coconut & Mango Tea cake A soft and flavorful tea cake with the tropical goodness of mango and coconut, topped...
contentHtml:
Coconut & Mango Tea cake

A soft and flavorful tea cake with the tropical goodness of mango and coconut, topped with whipped cream and toasted coconut flakes. Perfect for a cozy afternoon treat!

Recipe- Coconut & Mango Tea cake

Ingredients

  • Milk – 60 ml (1⁄4 cup)
  • Castor sugar – 125 grams (1 + 1⁄4 cup)
  • Oil – 60 ml (1⁄4 cup)
  • Mango pulp – 50 grams
  • Refined flour – 120 grams
  • Coconut powder – 40 grams
  • Baking powder – 1 tsp
  • Baking soda – 1⁄4 tsp

Instructions

  1. Combine milk, sugar, oil, and mango pulp.
  2. Add flour, desiccated coconut, baking powder, and baking soda, and mix into a
    smooth batter.
  3. Bake at 180°C until a skewer comes out clean.
  4. Top the cake with whipped cream and additional mango pulp, swirling to create a
    pattern using a skewer.
  5. Garnish the edges with toasted coconut flakes.
  6. Cut yourself a slice and enjoy!

title: 4-Ingredient Chocolate Peanut Butter Smoothie
excerptHtml: A rich, chocolatey smoothie bowl that tastes just like dessert! Packed with bananas, cocoa, and peanut butter, it’s the perfect...
contentHtml:
A rich, chocolatey smoothie bowl that tastes just like dessert! Packed with bananas, cocoa, and peanut butter, it’s the perfect wholesome weekend breakfast.

Recipe- 4-Ingredient Chocolate Peanut Butter Smoothie

Ingredients

  • 3 whole Frozen Bananas
  • 5 tbsp Peanut Butter
  • 2 tbsp Cocoa Powder
  • 4–5 Dates
  • 80–120 ml Milk (any milk of your choice)

Instructions

  1. Start by using some frozen bananas.
  2. Add creamy peanut butter, a few dates for sweetness, and cocoa powder.
  3. Pour in milk of your choice and blend everything together to make a thick smoothie.
  4. Pour the smoothie into a bowl.
  5. Top with your favorite toppings — bananas, chocolate chips, roasted hazelnuts, and
    chocolate granola.
  6. Serve immediately and enjoy this luscious, mousse-like smoothie bowl!

title: Jamun Coconut Smoothie Bowl
excerptHtml: Jamun Coconut Smoothie Bowl A refreshing summer smoothie bowl made with frozen kala jamun, coconut water, and yogurt — perfectly...
contentHtml:
Jamun Coconut Smoothie Bowl

A refreshing summer smoothie bowl made with frozen kala jamun, coconut water, and yogurt — perfectly sweet, tangy, and bursting with tropical flavor.

Recipe – Jamun Coconut Smoothie Bowl

Ingredients

  • 1⁄2 cup Coconut Water
  • 3⁄4 cup Frozen Jamun
  • 1⁄2 cup Yogurt / Plain Curd
  • 2 tsp Honey
  • 2 tbsp Coconut Flesh (Malai)

Instructions

  1. Start by using some fresh coconut water or replace with regular water if you prefer.
  2. Add deseeded and frozen kala jamun, a little yogurt or plain thick curd, and some honey to sweeten it.
  3. Add the malai from the tender coconut and blend this all till all the chunks are pureed.
  4. Remove the smoothie in a bowl and top with your favorite ingredients – I used tadgola, coconut chips, chia seeds, and some blueberries.

Tips:

  • Frozen fruit is what makes the smoothie nice and thick. If you don’t have time to freeze the fruit, simply throw in a few chunks of ice while blending.
  • If you don’t want to make a smoothie bowl, simply add more coconut water to make it thinner and drink it out of a glass!
  • You can use any toppings of your choice — seeds, nuts, fruits, nut butters, etc. as long as the flavors complement each other.
  • I have used honey as a sweetener, but you can substitute it with any kind of sugar as well.
  • While smoothie bowls are packed with fruits, seeds, and other nutrient-dense ingredients, they’re still high in calories and not suited for diabetics or those who are watching their weight. Consume in moderation.
  • If not serving immediately, cling wrap and refrigerate the smoothie.

title: Spiced Hot Chocolate
excerptHtml: Spiced Hot Chocolate Is it even Christmas without a cup of warm, and delicious hot chocolate? December is the month...
contentHtml:
Spiced Hot Chocolate

Is it even Christmas without a cup of warm, and delicious hot chocolate? December is the month for cosying up with friends and family, and a mug of hot chocolate makes it even better! There are so many ways to make hot chocolate and trust me I’ve tried them all, before finally settling on this as the BEST hot chocolate recipe. It’s creamy, silky smooth and intensely chocolatey with both cocoa and dark chocolate being used! The spices add just the right amount Marshallows round up the experience! 

Recipe – Spiced Hot Chocolate

Ingredients

  • Whole milk – 1 cup (240 ml) 
  • Sugar – 1 tbsp
  • Star anise – 1
  • Dark chocolate – 1/2 cup, chopped
  • Cocoa powder – 1 tbsp
  • Cinnamon powder – 1/2 tsp
  • A pinch of nutmeg powder

Instructions

  1. In a medium sauce pan, add milk and and star anise. Let it simmer for a few minutes then add the sugar, dark chocolate, cocoa powder, cinnamon powder and nutmeg powder. 
  2. Use a whisk to stir well. Make sure there are no lumps. 
  3. Bring the milk to a boil and keep stirring until all of the chocolate is melted. 
  4. Switch off the flame and pour into cups while still hot.
  5. Top with toasted marshmallows, whipped cream or chopped chocolate and serve hot.

title: Paan Truffles
excerptHtml: Paan Truffles Paan is an essential part of a classic Indian gastronomical experience. A lot of people still like to...
contentHtml:
Paan Truffles

Paan is an essential part of a classic Indian gastronomical experience. A lot of people still like to end their meals with a ‘meetha paan’. Now, I don’t do that every day but I do love to indulge in the flavours of fragrant gulkand and betel leaves once in a while. These paan truffles are a great combination of the aforementioned flavours in subtle proportions hitting just the right chords.

Makes 10-12 truffles

Ingredients

  • White chocolate/compound  -200 g
  • Fresh cream – 100 g
  • Paan leaves – 3-4
  • Gulkand – 50-60 g
  • Desiccated Dried Coconut – As needed

Instructions

  1. Measure and pour the cream in a heavy bottomed pan. Chop paan leaves and add them to the cream. On a low flame, bring the cream to a boil and simmer for 2-3 minutes. Switch off the flame and cover the pan for 10-15 minutes.
  2. Chop the white chocolate and melt it over a double boiler or if using microwave , in spurts of 15 seconds each until its completely melted and lumpfree.
  3. Strain the infused cream over the melted chocolate and whisk well.
  4. Refrigerate this ganache for at least 3-4 hours or overnight.
  5. Measure balls of 15 g each. Flatten each and place about 5-6 g gulkand between each ball and roll it up.
  6. Refrigerate the ball for 10 minutes.
  7. Melt the white chocolate and place the coconut in a small bowl or plate.
  8. Take out the ganache balls and dunk each into the white chocolate and then roll it over in the coconut to cover fully on all sides.
  9. Transfer each truffles onto a parchment lined plate/ tray and let it set for some time before serving.

title: Eggless Thandai Cake
excerptHtml: Eggless Thandai Cake Simple one bowl recipe for the fun festive fusion eggless Thandai cake that comes together in minutes....
contentHtml:
Eggless Thandai Cake

Simple one bowl recipe for the fun festive fusion eggless Thandai cake that comes together in minutes. Festive feels guaranteed!

If you are looking for a festive, delicious and flavourful fusion dessert, you’ve come to the right place. This thandai cake is juicy, slightly spicy and full of flavour Indian fusion cake that comes together in minutes and is an instant hit in any celebration.

This cake draws its inspiration from the very popular ‘Thandai’ which is typically a big hit during Holi and Mahashivratri. It is usually made with almonds, fennel seeds, rose petals, poppy seeds, cardamom, saffron milk and a few more nuts and spices. Needless to say it’s incredibly flavourful. This cake beautifully retains the flavour of Thandai in a soft and moist crumb which makes it simply irresistible.

Ingredients

  • Yogurt- 120 gm
  • Caster sugar- 100 gms
  • Oil- 90ml
  • Thandai Powder- 20gms
  • Thandai syrup- 40gms
  • Refined flour- 210gms
  • Baking powder- 1+1/4 tsp
  • Baking soda- ½ tsp
  • Milk- 80ml
  • Pistachios for topping

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 180 degree C.
  2. Grease a pan with oil/ butter.
  3. In a bowl, whisk sugar, oil and yogurt until light and fluffy.
  4. Add the thandai syrup and thandai powder and mix well.
  5. Now sieve the flour, baking powder, baking soda in the same bowl.
  6. Combine the ingredients while adding milk to it until it becomes a smooth batter. Do not overmix.
  7. Transfer the batter to a pan and top with chopped pistachios or any nuts of your choice.
  8. Bake for 45-50 minutes or till a toothpick comes out clean.
  9. Let it cool completely and refrigerate for a couple of hours before serving.

title: Eggless Biscoff Mufins

contentHtml:
Eggless Biscoff Mufins

Delicious and easy one bowl Biscoff muffins recipe for all the biscoff lovers out there. These have a tender crumb with an intense Lotus Biscoff flavor that is

Ingredients

  • Yogurt- 120 g
  • Castor sugar- 80 g
  • Oil- 60ml
  • Refined Flour- 140 g
  • Cocoa powder- 10 g
  • Baking powder- ½ tsp
  • Baking soda- ¼ tsp
  • Chocolate chips- 2-3 tbsp
  • Lotus biscoff spread- ½ cup

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven at 180 degrees and line a muffin pan with liners.

  5. Add a tbsp of batter in each muffin cavity followed by ½ tbsp of Biscoff spread. Top it up with another 1 tbsp of batter and ½ tbsp of Biscoff spread. Now with the help of a skewer, make swirls.
  6. Bake at 170 degrees for 18-20 minutes or till a toothpick inserted comes out

title: Eggless Mango Tart
excerptHtml: Eggless Mango Tart A summery eggless mango tart with a buttery shortcrust pastry and a smooth, homemade mango custard —...
contentHtml:
Eggless Mango Tart

A summery eggless mango tart with a buttery shortcrust pastry and a smooth, homemade mango custard — light, refreshing, and made completely from scratch.

About the Recipe

A summer tart is a thing of beauty — light, fruity, and elegant. This eggless mango tart has everything I love in a dessert: a crisp, buttery pastry base and a luscious mango custard that sets beautifully in the fridge. It’s made without eggs, custard powder, or condensed milk — just real ingredients and fresh mangoes. I often make this when mangoes are in season; it’s one of those desserts that looks fancy but is surprisingly easy to put together.

Ingredients

For the Tart Base
All-purpose flour – 1½ cups (210 g)
Castor sugar – ¼ cup (50 g)
Baking powder – a pinch
Cold butter (cut into cubes) – ¼ + ⅓ cup (135 g)
Ice-cold water – 2–4 tsp

For the Filling
Milk – 1 cup
Mango pulp – ½ cup
Castor sugar – ¼ cup
Cornflour – 5 tbsp
Vanilla extract – 1 tsp

Use fresh mangoes puréed in a mixer, or canned pulp if mangoes aren’t in season.

Instructions

For the Tart Shell

  1. Mix together flour, sugar, and baking powder in a bowl.

  2. Add cold butter cubes and rub them into the flour using only your fingertips until you get a breadcrumb-like texture.

  3. Gradually add 2 tsp ice-cold water to bring the dough together. Add more water only if needed; it should be firm, not sticky.

  4. Wrap the dough in cling film and refrigerate for 15–20 minutes.

  5. Preheat the oven to 170°C. Grease an 8-inch tart tin with butter.

  6. Roll out the chilled dough on a floured surface and gently transfer it to the tart tin, pressing along the edges. Dock the base with a fork or use parchment and baking beans.

  7. Bake for 25–30 minutes until lightly golden. Cool completely before adding the filling.

For the Filling

  1. In a heavy-bottomed pan, whisk together milk, mango pulp, sugar, and cornflour until lump-free.

  2. Place the pan on medium heat, stirring continuously until it thickens and just begins to boil.

  3. Turn off the heat, stir in the vanilla extract, and immediately pour the custard into the cooled tart shell.

  4. Smooth the top and refrigerate for at least 2 hours until set.

Notes & Tips

  • The tart can be made a day ahead — it actually tastes better when well chilled.

  • Garnish with whipped cream, chopped mangoes, mint leaves, or sliced nuts.

  • For variation, try the same recipe with strawberries or passionfruit purée.

  • Store refrigerated for up to 3 days.
